title: Fixation Protocol for Fresh Frozen Tissue Samples (post-MALDI)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"pbjo","text":"Scope:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6d46h","text":"To provide a method for removal of MALDI matrix, fixation and preparation of tissue sections for Cell DIVE.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"c5it4","text":"Special Notes:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"202of","text":"1. Make sure no tissue drying, particularly after matrix removal.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bv59k","text":"2. Glass petri dishes can be used for more fragile tissues.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6hng1","text":"3. This protocol can be used on Cell DIVE fresh frozen samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"ejpu8","text":"Paraformaldehyde should be used inside a chemical fume hood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1jv7f","text":"For research use only.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Milliq ddH2O","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":14,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5e8h1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7d321","text":"Solutions:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ed4n","text":"1.  1x PBS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6o6sm","text":"        100mL 10x PBS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3o79s","text":"        900mL ddH2O","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":17,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8bbnh","text":"        ---------------------------","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bpgio","text":"        1000 mL total solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdq84","text":"2.  Block Solution:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b6c3b","text":"        2.5 grams of BSA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fqdt0","text":"        5 mL of reconstituted donkey serum (reconstituted in 10mL of ddH2O)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":72,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9o3d7","text":"        0.25 mL of Tween 20","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7i8mu","text":"        44.75 mL of 1X PBS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2vhdl","text":"        ---------------------------","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71f6m","text":"        50 mL total block solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9r44s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: After the tissue has been imaged via MALDI IMS, the slide should be placed in a coplin jar or glass petri dish with a solution of room temperature 4% paraformaldehyde in PBS (*must be room temperature).
Place jar/dish on a shaker for 5 minutes.
Discard the PFA (the MALDI IMS matrix will come off the slide in this step).

Add fresh 4% PFA in PBS and shake for 5 more minutes (for a total of 10 minutes in PFA). Washing with PFA solution will fix and rehydrate the tissue as the matrix is removed. This preserves the tissue and antigens, and significantly reduces artifacts.
Add fresh PBS for 3 minutes on shaker. Repeat this wash two more times for a total of 3 times for 3 minutes each. pH should be neutral
Incubate the slides in 50 mg/mL bovie serum albumin (BSA), 10% (v/v) host-derived serum (depends on which antibodies they use), and 0.5% (v/v) Tween-20 in PBS for 30 minutes.

Be very careful. Tissue will be very fragile. This permeabilizes the tissue slowly. The Tween cannot be substituted for triton X, as triton X is too harsh and will degrade the tissue. 30 minutes works for most. Can do up to 2 hours before the tissue gets very damaged/adherence issues/weird background.
Add fresh PBS for 3 minutes on shaker. Repeat this wash two more times for a total of 3 times for 3 minutes each.
creator: Jamie Allen
